name: Multi-tier pagoda tower
description: A standalone temple pagoda: a square shaft of `tier_count` storeys, each storey smaller than the one below and crowned with a curved upturned-eave course, the whole topped with a finial mast. Every storey is a hollow room with a glazed window on each face, so it reads as a habitable East-Asian tower rather than a solid spire.
placement_rules: Square footprint preferred so the four corner horns stay symmetric.
Each upper storey is centred over the one below and strictly within it (no floating walls).
Stands free in a courtyard or temple precinct as a strong vertical centre.
lighting_ventilation: One glazed window per face per storey; the deep eaves shade each tier.

chinese.notes: Imperial red shaft with yellow-tiled tiers and gilt finial.

japanese.notes: Dark-timber shaft with charcoal tiers (Hōryū-ji feel).

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.pagoda_tower.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: East-Asian (Japanese/Chinese) motif. Coherent in japanese/chinese (and fantasy). Out of place in Western, Gothic, Renaissance, Modern, Mediterranean buildings — use only if the building is East-Asian.
